revision.comment: new article -- 1st steps in a constructive direction

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (February 9, 1966–  nbsp; )Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403] is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV] ==Early life== Breen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earnd a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996. ==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo. Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001 ==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities]: [http://orlabs.oclc.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ] {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Workshop'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honki Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen ; Articles
* "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto''," ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373-402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''onumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227-24. ==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]] ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2. {{Persondata | NAME = Teeuwen, Mark | ALTERNATIVE NAMES = | SHORT DESCRIPTION = | DATE OF BIRTH = | PLACE OF BIRTH = Netherlands | DATE OF DEATH = | PLACE OF DEATH = }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Teeuwen, Mark}} [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Japanologists]] [[Category:Living people]] {{academic-stub}} {{Japan-hist-stub}}

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (February 9, 1966–  nbsp; )Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403] is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV] ==Early life== Breen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earnd a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996. ==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo. Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001 ==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities]: [http://orlabs.oclc.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ] {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Workshop'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honki Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen ; Articles
* [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373-402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''onumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227-24. ==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]] ==Notes== {{Reflist}} ==References== * Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2. {{Persondata | NAME = Teeuwen, Mark | ALTERNATIVE NAMES = | SHORT DESCRIPTION = | DATE OF BIRTH = | PLACE OF BIRTH = Netherlands | DATE OF DEATH = | PLACE OF DEATH = }} {{DEFAULTSORT:Teeuwen, Mark}} [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Japanologists]] [[Category:Living people]] {{academic-stub}} {{Japan-hist-stub}}

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (born February 9, 1966)Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403] is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV] In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through. ==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996. ==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo. Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966)<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities]: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''onumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 21 April 1993, [[Laar]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices,3D printing and he is a Professor at the [[University of Teeuwen]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was a very talented footballer. At the age of 18 he played his first game for his local team [[sv.Laar]] scoring three times. Two years later he got a offer to play for proffesional football club [[Helmond sport]], but turned it down because of his knee injury. Becuase he didn't went proffecianl he could focus on his study, with great succes. Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 2014. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 2015.<ref name="oslo"/> He was voted personof the year in his hometown [[Laar]] in 2015. To honor him his first love, [[sv.laar]], hosted a football match with some iconic dutch football players. Mark played alongside [[Jaap Stam]], [[Tommy Lund]], [[Dirk Marcellis]], [[Oscar Moens]], [[ Boudewijn Zenden ]] and his father Henk Teeuwen.
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 21 April 1993, [[Laar]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices,3D printing and he is a Professor at the [[University of Teeuwen]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was a very talented footballer. At the age of 18 he played his first game for his local team [[SV Laar]] scoring three times. Two years later he got a offer to play for proffesional football club [[Helmond sport]], but turned it down because of his knee injury. Becuase he didn't went proffesional he could focus on his study, with great succes. Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 2014. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 2015.<ref name="oslo"/> He was voted personof the year in his hometown [[Laar]] in 2015. To honor him his first love, [[SV laar]], hosted a football match with some iconic dutch football players. Mark played alongside [[Jaap Stam]], [[Tommy Lund]], [[Dirk Marcellis]], [[Oscar Moens]], [[ Boudewijn Zenden ]] and his father Henk Teeuwen.
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

He is an expert in Japanese religious practices,3D printing and he is a Professor at the [[University of Teeuwen]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was a very talented footballer. At the age of 18 he played his first game for his local team [[SV Laar]] scoring three times. Two years later he got a offer to play for proffesional football club [[Helmond sport]], but turned it down because of his knee injury. Becuase he didn't went proffesional he could focus on his study, with great succes. Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 2014. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 2015.<ref name="oslo"/> He was voted personof the year in his hometown [[Laar]] in 2015. To honor him his first love, [[SV laar]], hosted a football match with some iconic dutch football players. Mark played alongside [[Jaap Stam]], [[Tommy Lund]], [[Dirk Marcellis]], [[Oscar Moens]], [[ Boudewijn Zenden ]] and his father Henk Teeuwen.
==Career== Since 2014, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Personal life== From 2014 through 2017, Teeuwen was a pubowner in his hometown [[Laar]]. He called the pub [[vultures]] after his two best friends who passed away in a [[carnavalsoptochongeluk]].
His lovelife is very controversiol even by [[Dutch]] standards. Pressumably it's because of his time he spend in [[Japan]]. There he got involved with [[geisha]]. He also was very critical and good musician. with his friend [[Andre Houtappels]] formed the band [[wils dich een kieer in mien eppelke biete]]. His band had many succeses and a couple of top ten hits in his own country. [[Do you like my apple?]] and [[rake with no snake]].
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

July 15, 2001</ref>

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the 3D printer'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen * ''Een Anus Bleken Met Low Budget'' (2010), with Bas Aendekerk
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

revision.text:
'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a [[Netherlands|Dutch]] academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a Professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called [http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape], he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a Lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{Authority control}}

He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://webcache.googleusercontent.com/search?q=cache:8cEW0upE3CwJ:www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf+mark+teeuwen&hl=en&gl=us faculty CV]</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS, Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61-2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

[http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{Authority control}}

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

revision.text:
'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{Authority control}}

Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. ; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

[[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{Authority control}}

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]

July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
