'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
[[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]]

July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

{{DEFAULTSORT:Teeuwen, Mark}} [[Category:1966 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Dutch Japanologists]] [[Category:Dutch academics]] [[Category:Leiden University alumni]] [[Category:Academic staff of the University of Oslo]] [[Category:Dutch expatriates in Norway]] [[Category:People from Eindhoven]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qqqqq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qqqqq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qqqqq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qqqqq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qqqqq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

{{DEFAULTSORT:Teeuwen, Mark}} [[Category:1966 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Dutch Japanologists]] [[Category:Dutch academics]] [[Category:Leiden University alumni]] [[Category:Academic staff of the University of Oslo]] [[Category:Dutch expatriates in Norway]] [[Category:People from Eindhoven]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Mark Teeuwen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

[http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.

==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.

==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]

==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.

Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
