revision.text:
'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

revision.text:
'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]

==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

revision.comment: Bot: Add Wikidata has short description - based on Wikidata English short description
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{draft categories|
[[Category:1966 births]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Dutch Japanologists]] [[Category:Dutch academics]] [[Category:Leiden University alumni]] [[Category:Academic staff of the University of Oslo]] [[Category:Dutch expatriates in Norway]]
}}
[[en:Mark Teeuwen]]
[[Category:Pages without translation templates]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[arz:مارك تيوين]] [[fr:Mark Teeuwen]] [[no:Mark Teeuwen]] {{wikidata link|Q6769960}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

July 15, 2001</ref>

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, [[:en:Eindhoven|Eindhoven]])<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the [[:en:University of Oslo|University of Oslo]].<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, [[:en:University of Wales|University of Wales]] in [[:en:Cardiff|Cardiff]]. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Rambelli, Fabio]]. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.
{{draft categories|
}}

Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. [[:en:Historicity|Historicity]] is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of [[:en:Toshio Kuroda|Toshio Kuroda]].<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, [[:en:OCLC|OCLC]]/[[:en:WorldCat|WorldCat]] encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with [[:en:Bernhard Scheid|Bernhard Scheid]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with [[:en:Fabio Rambelli|Fabio Rambelli]] * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

[http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of {{ill|Toshio Kuroda|en|Toshio Kuroda|WD=Q130309955|lt=Toshio Kuroda}}.<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|WD=Q190593|lt=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|WD=Q846596|lt=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|WD=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

==References== * {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Rambelli, Fabio}}. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, {{ill|Eindhoven|en|Eindhoven|WD=Q9832|lt=Eindhoven}})<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the {{ill|University of Oslo|en|University of Oslo|WD=Q486156|lt=University of Oslo}}.<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, {{ill|University of Wales|en|University of Wales|WD=Q1072029|lt=University of Wales}} in {{ill|Cardiff|en|Cardiff|WD=Q10690|lt=Cardiff}}. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. {{ill|Historicity|en|Historicity|WD=Q1517710|lt=Historicity}} is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of {{ill|Toshio Kuroda|en|Toshio Kuroda|WD=Q130309955|lt=Toshio Kuroda}}.<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|WD=Q190593|lt=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|WD=Q846596|lt=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|WD=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}
==References== * {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Rambelli, Fabio}}. [https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|WD=Q190593|lt=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|WD=Q846596|lt=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|WD=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, {{ill|Eindhoven|en|Eindhoven|WD=Q9832|lt=Eindhoven}})<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the {{ill|University of Oslo|en|University of Oslo|WD=Q486156|lt=University of Oslo}}.<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, {{ill|University of Wales|en|University of Wales|WD=Q1072029|lt=University of Wales}} in {{ill|Cardiff|en|Cardiff|WD=Q10690|lt=Cardiff}}. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. {{ill|Historicity|en|Historicity|WD=Q1517710|lt=Historicity}} is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of {{ill|Toshio Kuroda|en|Toshio Kuroda|WD=Q130309955|lt=Toshio Kuroda}}.<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|WD=Q190593|lt=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|WD=Q846596|lt=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|WD=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]
==Notes== {{Reflist}}

[https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001; book review excerpted from ''Monumenta Nipponica,'' 56:2.

==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|WD=Q190593|lt=OCLC|1=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|WD=Q846596|lt=WorldCat|1=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|WD=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid|1=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|WD=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli|1=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.
==See also== * [[Honji suijaku]]

[[en:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[arz:مارك تيوين]] [[fr:Mark Teeuwen]] [[no:Mark Teeuwen]] {{wikidata link|Q6769960}} [[Category:1966 births]] [[Category:Academic staff of the University of Oslo]] [[Category:Dutch academics]] [[Category:Dutch expatriates in Norway]] [[Category:Dutch Japanologists]] [[Category:Historians of Japan]] [[Category:Leiden University alumni]] [[Category:Living people]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[arz:مارك تيوين]][[en:Mark Teeuwen]][[fr:Mark Teeuwen]][[no:Mark Teeuwen]] '''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, {{ill|Eindhoven|en|Eindhoven|qid=Q9832|lt=Eindhoven|1=Eindhoven}})<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the {{ill|University of Oslo|en|University of Oslo|qid=Q486156|lt=University of Oslo|1=University of Oslo}}.<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, {{ill|University of Wales|en|University of Wales|qid=Q1072029|lt=University of Wales|1=University of Wales}} in {{ill|Cardiff|en|Cardiff|qid=Q10690|lt=Cardiff|1=Cardiff}}. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. {{ill|Historicity|en|Historicity|qid=Q1517710|lt=Historicity|1=Historicity}} is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of {{ill|Toshio Kuroda|en|Toshio Kuroda|qid=Q130309955|lt=Toshio Kuroda|1=Toshio Kuroda}}.<ref name="rambelli"/>
==Selected works== In a statistical overview derived from writings by and about Mark Teeuwen, {{ill|OCLC|en|OCLC|qid=Q190593|lt=OCLC|1=OCLC}}/{{ill|WorldCat|en|WorldCat|qid=Q846596|lt=WorldCat|1=WorldCat}} encompasses roughly 20 works in 60+ publications in 5 languages and 2,000+ library holdings .<ref>[http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm WorldCat Identities] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101230150412/http://www.oclc.org/research/activities/identities/default.htm |date=2010-12-30 }}: [http://www.worldcat.org/identities/lccn-no97-29403 Teeuwen, Mark ]</ref> {{dynamic list}} * ''Watarai Shintô: an Intellectual History of the Outer Shrine in Ise'' (1996) * ''Nakatomi Harae Kunge: Purification and Enlightenment in Late-Heian Japan'' (1998) * ''Shinto in History: Ways of the Kami'' (1999), with [[John Breen (scholar)|John Breen]] * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan 'honji suijaku' as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002), with {{ill|Bernhard Scheid|en|Bernhard Scheid|qid=Q4894232|lt=Bernhard Scheid|1=Bernhard Scheid}} * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as a Combinatory Paradigm'' (2002) * ''Tracing Shinto in the History of Kami Worship'' (2002) * ''Shinto, a Short History'' (2003) * ''Buddhas and Kami in Japan: Honji Suijaku as Combinatory Paradigm'' (2003), with {{ill|Fabio Rambelli|en|Fabio Rambelli|qid=Q5427860|lt=Fabio Rambelli|1=Fabio Rambelli}} * ''Shinto: een geschiedenis van Japanse goden en heiligdommen'' (2004) * ''The Culture of Secrecy in Japanese Religion'' (2006) * ''A New History of Shinto'' (2010), with John Breen
; Articles * [http://journals.cambridge.org/action/displayAbstract;jsessionid=8B9425546D9A6DD0324488F9EAA6E625.tomcat1?fromPage=online&aid=1185388 "Comparative perspectives on the emergence of ''jindō'' and ''Shinto'',"] ''Bulletin of SOAS'', Vol. 70, No. 2, 2007, pp. 373–402. * "''Kokugaku'' vs. Nativism," ''Monumenta Nipponica'' 61–2, Summer 2006, pp. 227–24.

'''Mark J. Teeuwen''' (Marcus Jacobus Teeuwen, born 9 February 1966, {{ill|Eindhoven|en|Eindhoven|qid=Q9832|lt=Eindhoven|1=Eindhoven}})<ref>Library of Congress authority file, [http://errol.oclc.org/laf/no97-29403.html Mark Teeuwen, no97-29403]</ref> is a Dutch academic and Japanologist. He is an expert in Japanese religious practices, and he is a professor at the {{ill|University of Oslo|en|University of Oslo|qid=Q486156|lt=University of Oslo|1=University of Oslo}}.<ref name="oslo">University of Oslo, [http://www.hf.uio.no/ikos/personer/vit/markt/Mark%2520Teeuwen%2520cv.pdf faculty CV]{{dead link|date=February 2024|bot=medic}}</ref> In a 2002 essay called From Jindō to Shinto: A Concept Takes Shape,<ref>{{Cite web |url=http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |title=Archived copy |access-date=2011-02-02 |archive-date=2011-07-19 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110719235902/http://nirc.nanzan-u.ac.jp/publications/jjrs/pdf/636.pdf |url-status=dead }}</ref> he traced the evolution of the term "Shinto" from the reconstructed pronunciation ''Jindō'' at the time of the [[Nihon Shoki]] until today, describing the changes its meaning has gone through.
==Early life== Teeuwen was awarded his MA at the [[:en:University of Leiden|University of Leiden]] in 1989. His earned a Ph.D. at Leiden in 1996.<ref name="oslo"/>
==Career== From 1994 through 1999, Teeuwen was a lecturer at the Japanese Studies Centre, {{ill|University of Wales|en|University of Wales|qid=Q1072029|lt=University of Wales|1=University of Wales}} in {{ill|Cardiff|en|Cardiff|qid=Q10690|lt=Cardiff|1=Cardiff}}. Since 1999, he has been Professor of Japanese at the University of Oslo.<ref name="oslo"/>
Teeuwen's critical examination of religious practices in Japan is considered ground-breaking. His published work has been informed by his historical research. {{ill|Historicity|en|Historicity|qid=Q1517710|lt=Historicity|1=Historicity}} is construed as a fundamental component of Teeuwen's view of Shinto.<ref name="rambelli">Rambelli, Fabio. [http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html "Dismantling stereotypes surrounding Japan's sacred entities,"] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101214170508/http://search.japantimes.co.jp/cgi-bin/fb20010715a2.html |date=2010-12-14 }} ''Japan Times.'' July 15, 2001</ref>
Teeuwen's work is influenced by the writings of {{ill|Toshio Kuroda|en|Toshio Kuroda|qid=Q130309955|lt=Toshio Kuroda|1=Toshio Kuroda}}.<ref name="rambelli"/>
